items.title: Defensive wing Jordan Walsh reportedly extends with Boston on three-year, $16 million deal
items.summary: Jordan Walsh already was going to be back in Boston next season, the team had picked up his $2.4 million option, but now he is going to be a fixture on the wing there for years.Walsh and the Celtics agreed to a three-year, $16 million extension, something first reported by NBA insider Chris Haynes and since confirmed by other reports. The first two years of this deal are fully guaranteed, with the third being a team option. This locks Walsh in with Boston for at least three more years at a very affordable price for a rotation player.Walsh played in 68 games last season for the Celtics, averaging 5.4 points and four rebounds a game, and his 3-point shot has improved, with him knocking down 38.4% from beyond the arc last season. However, his defense was really his calling card. He is a high-level on-ball defender on the wing, which is where Joe Mazzulla leaned into him.LOOK AT THE DEFENSE…

items.title: NBA Rookie Rankings: Who's at the head of the class after Summer League?
items.summary: With Summer League officially in the rearview mirror, it's a good time to build a list of what we just witnessed. The context of Summer League can lead to varied results. Bucket-getting tends to stand out, but it's also a chance to see the little things. Was there consistency throughout? Was there improvement? The fun of this exercise is the timing. In July, there is a chance for players to be highlighted in ways they may not be come the regular season. There are also highs and lows they have to work through in Summer League that may be made easier with a full roster in October.The one thing that's crystal clear right now: Rookie of the Year is going to be a tight, fun (and likely discourse-filled) race. The top four picks — AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson, Cameron Boozer, Caleb Wilson — all showcased why their teams should be excited. (Take a second to realize that one of them…

items.title: Why Knicks are poised to be regular season juggernaut in 2026-27
items.summary: While the high may still be coming down for Knicks fans experiencing their first championship in 53 years, the rest of the league is back to usual business, re-shuffling their rosters in pursuit of New York’s throne. Opposing fans are already touting their new acquisitions and downplaying the Knicks' run as flukey, but they may be in for a rude awakening.With how dominantly and cohesively New York ran through the playoffs, the league should be more fearful they’ll come in even better than they were last season. Here’s why we shouldn't be surprised if the Knicks continue their tear with a 60-win regular season.The manner in which New York steamrolled the playoffs - 12 consecutive wins, only three total losses by a combined six points, massive closeout blowouts and a historic point differential - suggests it wasn’t just a heater, but an all-time great team finding itself.…

items.summary: Mar 29, 2026; New Orleans, Louisiana, USA; LSU Tigers mens basketball head coach Will Wade looks on against the Houston Rockets during the first half at Smoothie King Center. Mandatory Credit: Stephen Lew-Imagn Images | Stephen Lew-Imagn Images LSU Tigers men’s basketball head coach Will Wade has a clear vision for what he wants the program to look like as they continue rebuilding the roster.Speaking about the roster overhaul with Brendon Fairbairn of Louisiana First News, Wade said the emphasis was on bringing in players with the size, athleticism and skill needed to compete in one of college basketball’s toughest conferences.“Long, athletic guys that can compete in the SEC,” Wade said. “You need some skill and shooting at the guard spot, and we feel like we’ve accomplished a lot of that.”The comments highlight the philosophy driving LSU’s roster construction throughout the beginning…

items.title: Brad Stevens locking up Celtics' fleet of young wings
items.summary: Brad Stevens locking up Celtics' fleet of young wings originally appeared on NBC Sports BostonBoston Celtics president of basketball operations Brad Stevens continues to lock up his team’s fleet of young wings.The Celtics reportedly agreed to a three-year, $15 million extension with 22-year-old Jordan Walsh. Boston previously picked up Walsh’s $2.4 million option for the 2026-27 season, and now has Walsh on the books through the 2029-30 season. HoopsHype reported the deal includes a third-year team option.Ron Harper Jr. inked a four-year, $13.7. million extension at the start of the summer. Baylor Scheierman is extension eligible next summer, while Hugo Gonzalez has three years remaining on his rookie deal. The Celtics will pay none of those players more than three percent of the salary cap in any of those seasons.For Walsh, there’s a bit of security after riding a bit of a playing…

items.title: LeBron decision drags on: Latest rumors, news on next team
items.summary: It's Day 24 and still no sign or peep from LeBron James about where his next NBA destination will be for his 24th season.If it feels like this process has been drawn out, it's because it kind of has. And that could be intentional.It's been thrown out into the realm of possibilities that James and company are purposefully stalling the decision to stick it to NBA commissioner Adam Silver after he pleaded for James to rush into his choice so the season schedule can be finalized and released.Cleveland sports radio personality Andy Baskin said Silver's comments "irritated" James."LeBron's crew was probably ready to make an announcement... they were probably comfortable making an announcement as early as last week that they were there (in New York) and then people were like telling them what to do," Baskin said. "There's a little bit of…

items.title: 3 Suns’ storylines already worth circling before training camp
items.summary: The Phoenix Suns have positioned themselves well heading into this upcoming season. With an underdog mentality last year, they proved the doubters wrong and exceeded every preseason expectation. This led them to the playoffs, and even if it was a sweep at the hands of the Oklahoma City Thunder, it was a refreshing year compared to some previous ones.By making this leap, though, it gave the fans something to root for and be excited for heading into this offseason. That is when we saw most of our players retained, along with some new faces, including Miles Bridges and Luke Kennard. That said, with this season full of excitement and the Summer League now over, the waiting period begins for all of us. So today we are going to dive in and explore three parts of this Suns team that I think will be exciting to dissect once the fall does eventually hit.1. The Center Rotation DebateObviously,…
